zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Day於1604年出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾吉爾本時，他的父親Richard Day29歲，母親Anna Kirby25歲。他於大約1630年與Mary在英國素福爾可易普斯維其結婚。他於1634年住在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可坎布利其。他去世於1648年5月20日之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦劍橋大學默里 倫敦安希特 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Day于1604年出生在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾吉爾本时，他的父亲Richard Day29岁，母亲Anna Kirby25岁。他于大约1630年与Mary在英国素福爾可易普斯維其结婚。他于1634年住在美国马萨诸塞州米德萊可坎布利其。他去世于1648年5月20日之后在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦劍橋大學默里 倫敦安希特 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Day
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English day(e), dey(e) ‘dairyman or dairymaid’. Originally used only of women, it was later used of men with the sense ‘man in charge of the dairy cattle’. This is probably the most common source of the surname.

English: from the Middle English personal name Day(e) or Dey. In western England this is probably a pet form of David , but in northern England and perhaps elsewhere also it is a late Middle English variant of Daw, a pet form of Ralph (see Daw , Dakin ).
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deaghaidh (see O'Dea ).

variants.name: Goody
meanings: from the Middle English salutation godne dæie ‘good day’, possibly applied as a nickname for someone who frequently used this greeting.
possibly in some instances from Middle English god(e)day(e) (or dey(e)) ‘good dairy man’ (see Day ).

variants.name: Days
meanings: English (Worcestershire and Warwickshire) and Welsh: variant of Day , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In the West Midlands this name commonly a variant of Dias 2.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Daymon
meanings: English (Cornwall): from a variant of the Middle English personal name Dayman, which has a number of explanations. It may be from an Old English Dægmann, perhaps a pet form of names in Dæg- ‘day’. Alternatively, the name may be a pet form of the Middle English personal name Day, to which the hypocoristic suffix -man has been added. Day is sometimes a short form of David but is also a 14th-century variant of Daw , a rhyming pet form of Ralph . The name may also be an occupational name for a dairyman, from Middle English daie ‘man in charge of dairy cattle’ + man ‘servant, worker’, similar in usage to Old English Flotmann ‘sailor’; or an occupational name for a ‘servant of a man named Day’. Daymon is also a variant of Diamond . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

variants.name: Diamond
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): from an Anglicized form of a metonymic occupational name for a diamond worker, derived from German Diamant, French and Dutch diamant ‘diamond’, or Yiddish dime(n)t, from medieval Latin diamas, genitive diamantis, and ultimately from Greek adamas ‘unconquerable’, genitive adamantos, a reference to the hardness of the stone.

English: from the rare Middle English female personal name Diamand, derived from Old French diamaunt, diamaund ‘diamond’, found in Middle English as diamound, deamaunt, and deamon. The name may alternatively be a variant of Daymon or a pet form of the Middle English personal name Day , to which the hypocoristic suffix -man has been added.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Diamáin ‘descendant of Diamán’, earlier Díomá or Déamán, a diminutive of Díoma, itself a pet form of Diarmaid (see McDermott ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Oldest Grave seen in the Memorials list.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Day was born in 1604, in Kilburn, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Day, was 29 and his mother, Anna Kirby, was 25. He married Mary about 1630, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England. He lived in Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States in 1634. He died after 20 May 1648, in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Day est né en 1604, à Kilburn, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Day, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Anna Kirby, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Mary environ 1630, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre. Il a vécu à Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis en 1634. Il est décédé après 20 mai 1648, à Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterré à Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Day 1604 in Kilburn, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Day 29 und seine Mutter Anna Kirby 25. Er hat um 1630 in Ipswich, Suffolk, England Mary geheiratet. 1634 hat er in Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb nach 20. Mai 1648 in Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Day è nato il nel 1604 nel Kilburn, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard Day, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Anna Kirby, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Mary circa 1630, nel Ipswich, Suffolk, England. Vissuto a Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1634. Morì dopo il 20 maggio 1648, nel Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolto a Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Dayが1604年にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアキルブルンで生まれた時，父Richard Dayは29歳，母Anna Kirbyは25歳であった。約1630年にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチでMaryと結婚した。彼は1634年にはアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスカンブリッジに住んでいた。彼は1648年5月20日以後にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンニュー ロンドンアンシエント セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Day(이)가 1604년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 킬번에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Day(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Kirby(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1630년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 1634년도에 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 캄브리지에서 살았다. 그는 1648년 5월 20일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 뉴 런던 앤시트 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Day nasceu em 1604, em Kilburn, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Day, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Anna Kirby, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary aproximadamente 1630, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ele viveu em Cambridge, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América em 1634. Ele faleceu após 20 de maio de 1648, em Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America, e foi sepultado em Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Day родился в 1604 г. в местности килбурн, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Day было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Kirby, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary около 1630 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия. Он жил в местности камбридж, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в 1634 году. Он умер после 20 май 1648 г. в местности хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика и был похоронен в местности ансиент кеметири, ню лондон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Day nació en 1604, en Kilburn, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Day, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Anna Kirby, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Mary aproximadamente 1630, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Vivió en Cambridge, Middlesex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos en 1634. Murió después de 20 de mayo de 1648, en Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultado en Ancient Cemetery, New London, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.
